summary:
The vaccine provides significant protection against serious cases of pneumococcal infection primarily affecting very young infants who are at high risk before they can receive PCV13 coverage through routine immunizations. This makes it crucially important for preventing life-threatening complications associated with these specific pathogen types during early childhood when immune systems may still be developing fully. Sentence count: 4

Agent2's reasoning (Round 2):
PPV-23 offers substantial benefits mainly towards reducing morbidity and mortality related to invasive diseases caused predominantly by non-covered serotypes after initial vaccination series completion around age two; thus focusing efforts here maximizes public health impact while minimizing unnecessary exposure later stages lacking direct benefit but potentially increasing adverse reactions post-primary schedule completion.

Summary: PPV-23 significantly reduces hospitalizations and fatalities linked to invasive pneumococcal illnesses targeting unvaccinated serotypes once primary immunity wanes typically between ages one-to-two-years-old making targeted administration critical prior completing full pediatric schedules avoiding potential side effects beyond that point. Sentence Count: 5
